title: Letters to the Editor: Trump’s comments on Robert Mueller’s death are petty and immoral
plaintext:
To the editor: I write this while trying to control my outrage. I’m a 78-year-old Vietnam veteran and Veterans of Foreign Wars member. Robert Mueller was an 81-year-old Vietnam veteran (“Former FBI Director Robert Mueller dies. Says Trump: ‘Good, I’m glad he’s dead,’” March 21). Among his military awards were the Bronze Star for valor and the Purple Heart.

President Trump is a 79-year-old draft dodger whose accomplishments include medals not earned and two impeachment proceedings he did earn. Mueller went into harm’s way demonstrating courage and selflessness. Trump has spent a lifetime deflecting blame and failure onto others.

Trump’s comments on Mueller’s death (“Good, I’m glad he’s dead”) are another low mark on a career filled with disgraceful behavior. They are petty, immoral and have no honor.

Mueller will be remembered as a war hero and dedicated public servant. Trump will be remembered as a small man and a travesty.

Dan Cunha, Anaheim

..

To the editor: Of all the words I can come up with regarding Trump’s comments on Mueller’s death, I think “disgusting” would be the only one printable in the Los Angeles Times.

John Powell, Downey
sentence: They are petty, immoral and have no honor.
context: They are petty , immoral and HNH .

title: Modi, Trump discuss importance of keeping Strait of Hormuz open
plaintext:
Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi and US President Donald Trump spoke by phone on Tuesday and discussed the situation in the Middle East, including the importance of keeping the Strait of Hormuz open, said US envoy to India Sergio Gor and Modi in separate posts on X.

This was the first call between the two leaders since the US and Israel launched co-ordinated attacks against Iran on February 28.

“India supports de-escalation and restoration of peace at the earliest. Ensuring that the Strait of Hormuz remains open, secure and accessible is essential for the whole world,” Modi said in his post.

The conflict has disrupted sectors from air travel to shipping and fuel supplies, including by the near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz, which serves as a conduit for 40% of India’s crude oil imports.

Modi told parliament on Monday that the crisis had triggered unprecedented challenges for India and added that the fundamentals of the Indian economy were strong and the country had adequate availability of petroleum, fertilisers and coal to weather trade and energy disruptions.

A White House official confirmed the call between Trump and Modi but did not comment on what was discussed.

Reuters
sentence: “India supports de-escalation and restoration of peace at the earliest.
context: “ India supports de - escalation and ROP at the earliest .

title: Kenya Airways reports increased flight demand due to the Iran war
plaintext:
Kenya Airways said on Monday demand for seats on its flights had jumped due to the effects of war in the Middle East, with most of the gains coming from Europe, the US and Asia.

The US-Israeli war on Iran has upset the global aviation industry, prompting some airlines to raise fares and change their schedules to reduce stops at airports in the Middle East or to cancel flights altogether.

Kenya Airways said demand for seats on its flights, known as load factor, was now almost at 100%, up from around 70% in January.

“We were like this... until February. Then it significantly increased. We reached up to 90% total, 90, 99,” George Kamal, acting CEO of Kenya Airways, told reporters.

“And so the most we see the gains are coming from Europe, from the US and Asia. Those routes are contributing positively, very positively, to our network now,” Kamal added, without giving any further details.

Kenya Airways has about 56 days of jet fuel supply and is making efforts to obtain more from India, its flight operations head Paul Njoroge said.

Reuters
sentence: We reached up to 90% total, 90, 99,” George Kamal, acting CEO of Kenya Airways, told reporters.
context: We reached up to 90 % total , 90 , 99 , ” George Kamal , acting CEO OKA , told reporters .

title: ‘This chapter has been special’: Kea Zawadi bids Algoa FM farewell
plaintext:
Kea Zawadi has announced she is leaving Algoa FM after three years.

During her time at the station, the media personality and actress hosted the Algoa FM Top 30 and week nights from 7pm to 10pm.

Reflecting on her journey on the show, Kea said she was excited about the new chapter in her life.

“This chapter has been so special to me. From the first show to now, it’s been a journey of growth, learning and connection. I’m incredibly grateful to everyone who listened, sent messages and allowed me to be part of their daily lives,” she said in a statement shared on her Instagram timeline.

“This is not a goodbye, just the beginning of a new chapter.”

Kea will do her final show on Algoa FM on March 24.

Another media personality who marked three years at Algoa FM this year is Carly Fields. She took to her timeline to reflect on her journey.

“Three years and still smiling. I love what I do and grateful I get to do what I love. Thank you, Algoa FM and Alfie Jay, for giving me this opportunity to live my dream every day,” she said.
sentence: From the first show to now, it’s been a journey of growth, learning and connection.

title: Accused Easey Street double killer to face rape charge
plaintext:
A rape charge has been reinstated by prosecutors against the accused Easey Street double killer, after a magistrate struck out the offence.

Perry Kouroumblis, 66, has been committed to stand trial in the Supreme Court over the alleged 1977 murders of Suzanne Armstrong, 28, and Susan Bartlett, 27.

He was initially charged with two counts of murder and one of rape, known as carnal knowledge without consent in the 1970s.

However, magistrate Brett Sonnet discharged the rape charge in November, following a committal hearing.

Prosecutor Patrick Bourke SC informed the Supreme Court on Tuesday that a fresh indictment had been filed in the higher court, with two counts of murder and one of rape.

Kouroumblis has pleaded not guilty and denies all offences.

During the brief hearing, Kouroumblis' barrister Dermot Dann KC said his client maintained "he was not responsible for the killing of the two persons".

He said the fresh indictment was "something that needed to be considered" by the defence ahead of pre-trial hearings.

Bodies of the two women were found by their neighbours inside their Easey Street home in Collingwood, inner-Melbourne, on January 13, 1977.

1800 RESPECT (1800 737 732)

title: A 32-year-old man charged after police found machinery on Nambeelup property allegedly stolen from grain site
plaintext:
A 32-year-old man has been arrested after police discovered machinery worth $60,000 on a Nambeelup property that is alleged to have been stolen from a Wheatbelt grain site in February.

Officers from Boddington, Williams and Dwellingup police executed a search warrant at a property in Nambeelup, east of Mandurah, on March 20.

During the search they discovered a yellow trailer-mounted lighting tower and a yellow skid steer, two of the three vehicles that had been allegedly stolen from a grain service site on Coalfields Road in Arthur River earlier this year.

Between 6pm on January 31 and 6.30am on February 1, an unknown person entered the grain service and allegedly stole a Caterpillar Skid Steer 2015 model loader and loaded it onto a trailer before leaving in an unknown vehicle.

A few days later, between 5pm on February 3 and 5.30am on February 4, an unknown person forced entry into the same site and stole two light towers.

After locating the items, police seized the two machines and returned them to the grain service.

A man from Madora Bay was charged with one count of possession of stolen or unlawfully obtained property, and one count of receiving.

He is due to appear in Mandurah Magistrates Court on April 14.

Police say the investigation remained ongoing.

Anyone with any information in relation to this incident is asked to contact Crime Stoppers online via crimestopperswa.com.au or by calling 1800 333 000.

Reports can be made anonymously.
sentence: Officers from Boddington, Williams and Dwellingup police executed a search warrant at a property in Nambeelup, east of Mandurah, on March 20.
context: Officers from Boddington , Williams and Dwellingup police executed a search warrant at a property in Nambeelup , EOM , on March 20 .

title: Identity of man accused of encouraging ex-colleagues to die by suicide and sending their details to funeral homes revealed
plaintext:
The identity of a man accused of giving the details of his former colleagues to funeral homes after encouraging them to die by suicide has been revealed.

Adrian Bruno, 38, is accused of sending “graphic and confronting” emails to some of his former female colleagues.

Some of the threatening emails allegedly encouraged the women to die by suicide, police allege.

Mr Bruno is further accused of giving his alleged victims’ contact details to funeral homes, which then contacted the women to make arrangements for their funeral services.

The 38-year-old fronted Burwood Local Court for the first time on Tuesday morning.

Clutching a bible, Mr Bruno asked for an adjournment to seek legal advice.

His request was granted, with his matter to return to court on April 14 when he is due to enter pleas.

“I can leave? I can go home?” Mr Bruno asked the registrar.

Mr Bruno declined to comment on the case to journalists outside court, telling them “I’m seeking legal advice”.

He was arrested on March 16 and charged with 17 counts of using a carriage service to menace/harass/offend, use carriage service with intent for another to commit suicide, publish etc false misleading material to obtain advantage and destroy or damage property.

Mr Bruno will remain on bail ahead of his next court appearance, with interim apprehended violence orders to continue until that date.
sentence: Mr Bruno is further accused of giving his alleged victims’ contact details to funeral homes, which then contacted the women to make arrangements for their funeral services.
context: Mr Bruno is further accused of giving his alleged victims ’ contact details to funeral homes , which then contacted the women to make arrangements for TFS .

title: NJ man crawls through window, attempts to sexually assault girl before being subdued by resident: police
plaintext:
A New Jersey man allegedly broke into a home through a bathroom window and tried to sexually assault a girl as she slept early Saturday before he was subdued by the resident inside.

Jermin Fordyce, 36, is charged with first-degree counts of home invasion, burglary and attempted aggravated sexual assault, as well as second-degree aggravated assault, the Bergen County Prosecutor’s Office said.

Hackensack police officers responded to a home around 3:40 a.m. for a home invasion burglary. Once at the scene, they found Fordyce, who had been subdued by the residents inside the home after they found him inside one of the bedrooms, police said.

Fordyce entered the home through a bathroom window and entered the bedroom of the girl who was sleeping, officials said. Police described the victim only as a "resident." NJ.com reported the victim was a girl.

Once inside the room, Fordyce took off his pants and climbed on top of the girl as she was sleeping and attempted to sexually assault her, police said.

He allegedly strangled her during the assault, authorities said.

One of the other residents awoke to the sounds of a struggle coming from the girl's room and took down Fordyce.

Fordyce remains in police custody pending a court hearing.
sentence: A New Jersey man allegedly broke into a home through a bathroom window and tried to sexually assault a girl as she slept early Saturday before he was subdued by the resident inside.
context: A New Jersey man allegedly broke into a home through ABW and tried to sexually assault a girl as she slept early Saturday before he was subdued by the resident inside .

title: California cemetery removes banner draped over 9/11 memorial advertising new Islamic section after backlash
plaintext:
A cemetery in the San Francisco Bay Area has removed a banner draped across a 9/11 memorial that advertised a new Islamic section after backlash from the community.

NBC Bay Area reported that the owner of Memorial Gardens Cemetery said that he recently sold part of the property to a Muslim family, and that "he gave them permission to put up signage but did not authorize them to drape it over the 9/11 installation."

The owner reportedly had the signage removed, but community residents said that the sign should not have been erected to begin with.

NBC Bay Area reported that Danny Kimmel, a resident of Concord, California, said the banner advertising an Islamic memorial garden draped over the 9/11 memorial was placed in front of the cemetery where his mother was laid to rest.

"I felt a punch to the gut type of thing," he said, according to the report. "To see that sign on that memorial is kind of nutty is my thoughts."

Kimmel’s brother was killed in Vietnam, and he said his mother would be totally against the placement of the banner.

"She wouldn’t just roll over — she’d get up and walk," he said.

Violet Kimmel, Danny Kimmel’s sister-in-law, also shared concerns.

"There’s room for everybody, but just have a little respect where you’re going to put your things."

Fox News Digital reached out to Memorial Gardens Cemetery for comment.

"

Kimmel’s brother was killed in Vietnam, and he said his mother would be totally against the placement of the banner.
context: " Kimmel ’s brother was KIV , and he said his mother would be totally against the placement of the banner .

title: Human Rights Day: Remembering the Sharpeville massacre [VIDEO]
plaintext:
Some want Human Rights Day to be renamed Sharpeville Day, so the massacre of 1960 is remembered

Human Rights Day on 21 March is a national public holiday that commemorates the Sharpeville massacre of 1960.

It is a day of reflection on the country’s struggle for democracy, the sacrifices made to end apartheid and the commitment to upholding the rights enshrined in the Constitution.

Political leaders, community members and families of those who were killed in the massacre gathered at Sharpeville in the Vaal for a wreath-laying ceremony and commemoration through the singing of struggle songs, lighting candles and speeches in memory of those who passed away on the day.

“I find it hard to believe why our government named this day Human Rights Day when at the same time 32 years down the line our people’s human rights are being violated,” ActionSA president Herman Mashaba said.

“So I’m really calling upon the government to start looking at this particular matter, this day has got to be Sharpeville Day to make sure our grandchildren and future generations can always remember the day because we can’t build a nation when people don’t know their history.”
sentence: “So I’m really calling upon the government to start looking at this particular matter, this day has got to be Sharpeville Day to make sure our grandchildren and future generations can always remember the day because we can’t build a nation when people don’t know their history.”
context: “ So I ’m really calling upon the government to start looking at TPM , this day has got to be Sharpeville Day to make sure our grandchildren and future generations can always remember the day because we ca n’t build a nation when people do n’t know their history . ”

title: Trump orders War Dept to postpone strikes on Iranian energy sites, citing 'productive' talks to end war
plaintext:
President Donald Trump, in an all-caps post early Monday morning, declared progress toward "resolution" of the war on Iran.

"I AM PLEASE TO REPORT THAT THE UNITED STATES OF AMERICA, AND THE COUNTRY OF IRAN, HAVE HAD, OVER THE LAST TWO DAYS, VERY GOOD AND PRODUCTIVE CONVERSATIONS REGARDING A COMPLETE AND TOTAL RESOLUTION OF OUR HOSTILITIES IN THE MIDDLE EAST," Trump wrote on Truth Social.

"BASED ON THE TENOR AND TONE OF THESE IN DEPTH, DETAILED, AND CONSTRUCTIVE CONVERSATIONS, WITCH WILL CONTINUE THROUGHOUT THE WEEK, I HAVE INSTRUCTED THE DEPARTMENT OF WAR TO POSTPONE ANY AND ALL MILITARY STRIKES AGAINST IRANIAN POWER PLANTS AND ENERGY INFRASTRUCTURE FOR A FIVE DAY PERIOD, SUBJECT TO THE SUCCESS OF THE ONGOING MEETINGS AND DISCUSSIONS."
sentence: President Donald Trump, in an all-caps post early Monday morning, declared progress toward "resolution" of the war on Iran.
context: President Donald Trump , in an all - caps post EMM , declared progress toward " resolution " of the war on Iran .

title: Perth traffic: Wanneroo Road, Kwinana Freeway crashes causing peak hour headaches on Monday afternoon
plaintext:
A crash on Wanneroo Road northbound in Tuart Hill is causing peak hour headaches for drivers, with heavy congestion plaguing the major road on Monday.

The crash, reported by Main Roads at 3.56pm, closed the left lane of Wanneroo Road at Morley Drive.

Police and ambulance services were reported on site.

Traffic is congested and slow leading up to the site, and Main Roads has reminded motorists to allow for safe merging on approach.

A second crash on Kwinana Freeway northbound in Baldivis temporarily closed the major road earlier on Monday afternoon.

The crash, reported by Main Roads on Monday, occurred on the northbound exit to Safety Bay Road, and initially closed the exit.

The road has since been reopened, but the emergency lane of the freeway was temporarily blocked.

Emergency and towing services were pictured on the scene.

The crash has since been cleared.
sentence: The crash, reported by Main Roads on Monday, occurred on the northbound exit to Safety Bay Road, and initially closed the exit.
context: The crash , reported by Main Roads on Monday , occurred on the northbound exit to Safety Bay Road , and initially CTE .

title: Desperate search resumes for man who vanished in waters off NSW south coast
plaintext:
A desperate multi-agency search has resumed for a missing man who vanished in the waters off a regional town on the NSW south coast.

Emergency services rushed to Pacificana Drive in Sussex Inlet, near Jervis Bay, about 6.30pm on Sunday.

Police located a 29-year-old woman who required medical treatment.

She has been transported to Shoalhaven District Memorial Hospital in a stable condition.

The woman had been with a 25-year-old man before emergency services arrived, but he was not found on Sunday.

A NSW police spokeswoman said the search for the man resumed on Monday morning.

“(The) search resumed at 7am,” she said.

Officers are being assisted in their efforts by Surf Life Saving and Marine Rescue NSW.

The search area spans from Wreck Bay to Bendalong Point.

Police are urging anyone with information regarding the man’s whereabouts to contact Crime Stoppers on 1800 333 000.
sentence: The woman had been with a 25-year-old man before emergency services arrived, but he was not found on Sunday.
context: The woman had been with a 25 - year - old man before emergency services arrived , but he was not FOS .

title: Former Google executive Matt Brittin named new BBC director-general, The Times reports
plaintext:
March 22 (Reuters) - Former Google executive Matt Brittin has been confirmed as the BBC's new director-general, the Times reported on Sunday, citing sources.

Brittin's appointment was approved by the BBC board at a meeting on Thursday, with an official announcement expected this week, the report said.

Reuters could not immediately verify the report. BBC did not immediately respond to a request for a comment.

In January, BBC said Director-General Tim Davie would step down on April 2 and be temporarily succeeded by Rhodri Talfan Davies, the broadcaster's director of nations, until a permanent replacement is appointed.

Davie announced his resignation in Novemberfollowing criticism of the British corporation's news service over the misleading editing of a video clip of U.S. President Donald Trump.

(Reporting by Angela Christy in Bengaluru; Editing by Rashmi Aich and Sumana Nandy)
sentence: Davie announced his resignation in Novemberfollowing criticism of the British corporation's news service over the misleading editing of a video clip of U.S. President Donald Trump.
context: Davie announced his resignation in Novemberfollowing criticism of the British corporation 's news service over the misleading editing of AVC of U.S. President Donald Trump .

title: Suns to fight gun forward's rough conduct ban
plaintext:
Gold Coast will challenge the two-match ban handed to young gun Bailey Humphrey for rough conduct.

The 21-year-old forward was charged over a sling tackle on Richmond's Maurice Rioli during the Suns' comfortable 68-point win on Saturday.

The AFL match review officer graded the incident as careless conduct, high impact and high contact.

Gold Coast coach Damien Hardwick was quick to defend his player, telling reporters post-match that Humphrey should not have faced any sanction.

"No, I wouldn't have thought so. It should have been holding the ball," Hardwick said.

As it stands, Humphrey is set to miss the Suns' clashes with Melbourne and Sydney after the bye in round three.

The Demons were one of several Victorian clubs interested in recruiting Humphrey when he explored his options for a trade to his home state last year.

Humphrey met with Melbourne coach Steven King but Gold Coast held the player to his contract, which runs until the end of 2028.

Already without Brownlow medallist Matt Rowell (finger), the Suns have lost gun recruit Christian Petracca (hamstring) for the Dees clash and will be desperate to clear Humphrey at the tribunal.

One thing in Humphrey's favour in the hearing will be the fact Rioli was not seriously hurt in the sling tackle.

"He was fine. He played out the game ... and he was one of our better contributors in the last quarter," Tigers coach Adem Yze said post match.
sentence: He played out the game ... and he was one of our better contributors in the last quarter," Tigers coach Adem Yze said post match.
context: He played out the game ... and he was one of our better contributors in TLQ , " Tigers coach Adem Yze said post match .

title: American dream run: no sign of Aussie tennis ace waking
plaintext:
Talia Gibson shows no sign of waking from her American dream run as the red-hot Australian downed yet another top-20 victim at the Miami Open.

A day after sending four-time grand slam champ Naomi Osaka packing, the 21-year-old has outclassed one of the sport's brightest new talents, teenage world No.17 Iva Jovic 6-2 6-2 on Sunday.

It was Gibson's fifth victory over a top-20 player in just three weeks as she followed up her brilliant run to the quarter-finals at Indian Wells by reaching the last-16 in the second leg of the 'Sunshine swing' in the US.

There's no sign of the Perth prospect letting up either as she produced an immaculate display of serving, not getting broken once and not even giving up a break point opportunity as she dispatched the 18-year-old American Jovic in just 72 minutes.

Gibson came through qualifying in California, winning six matches in all, and has now won five on the trot in the other major WTA 1000 event. She's not dropped a set yet in Miami and hasn't even had her serve broken in the last three matches.

Before Indian Wells, the Australian had been ranked No.112 in the world. Having jumped to No.68, now she's set to at least become world No.56 next week and move to second in the Australian rankings ahead of Daria Kasatkina and behind only Maya Joint.

But the tests only get harder. In the last 16, she's now going to have to face Australian Open champion and world No.2 Elena Rybakina, who defeated Ukraine's Marta Kostyuk 6-3 6-4.
sentence: Talia Gibson shows no sign of waking from her American dream run as the red-hot Australian downed yet another top-20 victim at the Miami Open.
context: Talia Gibson shows no sign of waking from her American dream run as the red - hot Australian downed yet another top-20 victim at TMO .

title: A strike on a hospital in Sudan killed at least 64 people, WHO says
plaintext:
CAIRO — At least 64 people were killed, including at least 13 children, in a strike on a hospital in Sudan’s western Darfur region last week, the World Health Organization said.

The strike on the Al Daein Teaching Hospital in East Darfur on Friday also injured at least 89 people and rendered the hospital nonfunctional, WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said on X.

Sudan slid into chaos in April 2023 when a power struggle between the military and the rival paramilitary Rapid Support Forces exploded into war throughout the country.

The RSF has blamed the military for the strike on the hospital.

The army has denied the attack, but two military officials said the strike was targeting a nearby police station. They spoke on condition of anonymity as they were not allowed to discuss the matter openly.

The devastating war has killed more than 40,000 people, according to United Nations figures, but aid groups say that is an undercount and the true number could be many times higher.

The WHO has said that more than 2,000 people have been killed in attacks on medical facilities since the start of the war.

“Enough blood has been spilled. Enough suffering has been inflicted. The time has come to de-escalate the conflict in Sudan,” Tedros said.

Abuelgasim writes for the Associated Press. AP writer Yassir Abdalla in Shendi, Sudan, contributed to this report.
sentence: The RSF has blamed the military for the strike on the hospital.
context: The RSF has blamed the military FTS on the hospital .

title: Jolimont: Police urgently appeal to public for help to locate missing woman
plaintext:
WA Police have issued an urgent appeal for public assistance to locate a missing 81-year-old woman last seen in Perth’s western suburbs on Sunday morning.

Beverley Downsborough was last seen about 9am on March 22 in the vicinity of Rosebery Street, walking towards Hay Street in Jolimont.

Police hold serious concerns for her welfare.

Ms Downsborough is described as having a slim build, fair skin and grey-white shoulder-length hair. She was last seen wearing a deep navy-blue and white striped long-sleeve T-shirt, long black pants and black slip-on shoes.

Authorities say Ms Downsborough is living with Alzheimer’s disease and may be confused or unaware that she is lost.

Residents in Jolimont and surrounding areas are being urged to check their backyards, sheds and garages, as well as nearby vacant blocks and thoroughfares.

Anyone who sees Ms Downsborough or has information about her whereabouts is urged to contact police immediately on 131 444.
sentence: WA Police have issued an urgent appeal for public assistance to locate a missing 81-year-old woman last seen in Perth’s western suburbs on Sunday morning.
context: WA Police have issued an urgent appeal for public assistance to locate a missing 81 - year - old woman last seen in Perth ’s western suburbs OSM .

plaintext:
Two competing narratives have emerged since KwaZulu-Natal police chief Nhlanhla Mkhwanazi alleged in July 2025 that a cartel had penetrated South Africa’s criminal justice system, politics, and private security. title: Lebanon-Israel talks stall amid ongoing fire: The details
plaintext:
Lebanon is negotiating with Israel to halt hostilities, but progress remains stalled.

U.S. Ambassador to Lebanon Michel Issa has weighed in on the situation, but Lebanon’s position appears firm.

Sources told LBCI that President Joseph Aoun continues to insist that no negotiations take place before a cease-fire is established, as outlined in the initiative he launched last week to reduce escalation.

Parliament Speaker Nabih Berri also maintains his opposition to negotiating under fire. He has reiterated calls to return to the cease-fire agreement and implement it, emphasizing that any negotiations should occur within the framework of the mechanism committee, as before.

Meanwhile, Prime Minister Nawaf Salam has previously stated he is open to discussing any agenda, format, or location for talks with Israel.

Ultimately, negotiations between Israel and Lebanon have reached a near deadlock. Current developments are largely determined on the ground, and a ceasefire in Lebanon appears unlikely until the situation involving Iran becomes clearer.
sentence: He has reiterated calls to return to the cease-fire agreement and implement it, emphasizing that any negotiations should occur within the framework of the mechanism committee, as before.
context: He has reiterated calls to return to the cease - fire agreement and implement it , emphasizing that any negotiations should occur within the framework of TMC , as before .

title: Red-hot Green on verge of Aussie golfing first
plaintext:
Hannah Green sits on the cusp of a golfing first entering her Australian WPGA Championship final round at Sanctuary Cove.

The world No.7 (14 under) held a two-shot overnight lead and boasts wins at the Australian Open and Singapore's Women's World Championship - her seventh LPGA title - in her last two starts.

A hat-trick on the Gold Coast on Sunday would set Green apart as the first Australian to win three consecutive titles in major-tour events.

The West Australian and one-time major winner has had the renovated Pines layout at her mercy over the last three days, threatening to pull away from the field with early birdie runs on both Friday and Saturday.

But some rare errors kept her rivals interested, with South Africa's Casandra Alexander and Swiss talent Vanessa Knecht two back after Saturday's third round.

Golfers from Finland, India, Germany, England and Italy occupy the top-10 of a truly global field.

The $600,000 event, co-sanctioned with the Ladies European Tour, is being contested for just the second time and first since 2022 after the threat of a cyclone forced its cancellation last year.

Early movers on Sunday included Singapore's Shannon Tan and Brit Caley McGinty, who had both picked up three strokes to move to six under through five holes.

Raegan Denton was the next-best Australian, just outside the top-10 at seven under for the tournament after an early birdie to begin her final round.
sentence: Early movers on Sunday included Singapore's Shannon Tan and Brit Caley McGinty, who had both picked up three strokes to move to six under through five holes.
context: Early movers on Sunday included Singapore 's Shannon Tan and BCM , who had both picked up three strokes to move to six under through five holes .

title: Tigers top prospect goes viral for unfiltered reaction to Pirates' Seth Hernandez's 102 mph heater
plaintext:
Detroit Tigers top prospect Max Clark had a very honest reaction after seeing a 102.4 mph fastball on the first pitch of the game.

Clark was the leadoff batter for the Tigers in their 8-7 loss to the Pirates in their Spring Breakout game and was up against top prospect Seth Hernandez. Hernandez’s first pitch was a 102.4 mph fastball above the zone, and it got Clark’s attention.

"What the f---," Clark could be seen saying after the pitch went by him.

Clark, 21, ended up walking in the at-bat. Hernandez ended up inducing an inning-ending ground ball double play to conclude his lone inning of the game.

The game between the Tigers and Pirates was part of MLB’s Spring Breakout, which is an annual four-day event with exhibition games between the top prospects from all of MLB’s teams during spring training.

Clark is the No. 2 overall prospect in the Tigers farm system, according to MLB.com. Last season at High-A and Double-A, Clark hit .271 with 14 home runs and 67 RBI, and could make his big league debut this season, where he will see a lot of high-octane heat like Hernandez’s.

Hernandez, 19, was drafted by the No. 6 overall pick by the Pirates in last year’s draft. Hernandez already has a legitimate four-pitch mix and will begin his season in rookie ball.

The Pirates organization hopes that Hernandez, alongside top pitching prospect Bubba Chandler, will slot in behind National League Cy Young winner Paul Skenes atop their rotation for years to come.
sentence: Clark was the leadoff batter for the Tigers in their 8-7 loss to the Pirates in their Spring Breakout game and was up against top prospect Seth Hernandez.
context: Clark was the leadoff batter FTT in their 8 - 7 loss to the Pirates in their Spring Breakout game and was up against top prospect Seth Hernandez .

title: Mampara of the week: Sgt Fannie Nkosi
plaintext:
Sgt Fannie Nkosi had it coming when advocate Sesi Baloyi reprimanded him at the Madlanga commission for prevaricating, stonewalling and generally being economical with the truth during his week of testimony.

The serial motorbike buyer admitted to having a close relationship with, and receiving money from, criminal suspects such as Vusimusi “Cat” Matlala, and to sharing confidential police files with the late taxi tycoon Jotham Zanemvula “King Mswazi” Msibi.

His attempts at explaining away his indiscretions grew ever more fanciful, culminating in his imaginative invention of the fictional local authority of “Mathews Phosa municipality” in Mpumalanga, where his security boss brother was apparently bidding for tenders.

How long before Sgt Mampara swaps his blue uniform for a baggy orange one?

title: AFL 2026: GWS Giants’ clash with St Kilda delayed due to double lightning strike
plaintext:
Inclement weather has delayed the start of the AFL game between GWS and St Kilda at Engie Stadium in western Sydney.

The match was due to start at 415pm (AEDT).

Lightning flashed in the vicinity in the leadup to the scheduled start, forcing a 30-minute delay. Further lightning strikes during that initial delay have pushed the start time further back.

Bounce down has now been scheduled for 5.05 (AEDT)

The person in control of the music within the stadium showed a sense of humour by putting on the AC/DC standard Thunderstruck.
sentence: Lightning flashed in the vicinity in the leadup to the scheduled start, forcing a 30-minute delay.

title: Chuck Norris,  star of ‘Walker, Texas Ranger’, dies at 86
plaintext:
Action star Chuck Norris has died, his family said in a social media post on Friday.

“It is with heavy hearts that our family shares the sudden passing of our beloved Chuck Norris yesterday morning,” Norris’s family said in an Instagram post.

“While we would like to keep the circumstances private, please know that he was surrounded by his family and was at peace. To the world, he was a martial artist, actor and a symbol of strength. To us, he was a devoted husband, a loving father and grandfather, an incredible brother and the heart of our family.

“He lived his life with faith, purpose and an unwavering commitment to the people he loved. Through his work, discipline and kindness, he inspired millions around the world and left a lasting impact on so many lives. While our hearts are broken, we are deeply grateful for the life he lived and for the unforgettable moments we were blessed to share with him,” it continued.

Norris, who died at 86, was an ’80s action-film hero who fought the bad guys in Code of Silence, Missing in Action and The Delta Force and upheld the law in the TV series Walker, Texas Ranger.

The six-time undefeated World Professional Middleweight Karate Champion, whose tough-guy image inspired satirical “facts” that made him an internet phenomenon, had been hospitalised in Hawaii on Thursday, Variety reported.

Reuters
sentence: Norris, who died at 86, was an ’80s action-film hero who fought the bad guys in Code of Silence, Missing in Action and The Delta Force and upheld the law in the TV series Walker, Texas Ranger.
context: Norris , who died at 86 , was an ’ 80s action - film hero who fought the bad guys in COS , Missing in Action and The Delta Force and upheld the law in the TV series Walker , Texas Ranger .

title: Rankine will be better for the run: Crows coach
plaintext:
A rusty Izak Rankine almost pinched it.

Adelaide's dynamo added to his highlight reel with a spectacular late goal to steal the lead over the Western Bulldogs.

But the Dogs stole it right back for a six-point win -- 14.10 (94) to 13.10 (88) -- at Adelaide Oval that coach Luke Beveridge rated full of integrity.

His Adelaide counterpart, Matthew Nicks, was left ruing how a Rankine-capped burst of five goals in 10 minutes couldn't steal the win.

"It's great to get him back out there," Nicks said of Rankine's first game since serving a four-match ban for a homophobic slur.

"I think he'd be the first one to say it wasn't necessarily his best.

"But what do you expect from a guy who hasn't played footy for so long?"

Rankine had a touch of the fumbles in his 16-disposal return, before his late strike lifted the Crows.

"He had a few real key moments, which is typical Izak," Nicks said.

"He can do stuff that a lot of guys can't. And he'll be better for the run.

"It was nice to see him back out there, in amongst the group. He has been been leading off-field, so it was a chance for him to get on-field and get amongst it."

Adelaide's prized recruit Callum Ah Chee sufferied another hamstring injury and faces a lengthy stint on the sidelines.

Ah Chee initially hurt the same hamstring playing for Western Australia against Victoria in State of Origin.
sentence: Ah Chee initially hurt the same hamstring playing for Western Australia against Victoria in State of Origin.
context: Ah Chee initially hurt the same hamstring playing FWA against Victoria in State of Origin .

title: Prep sports roundup: Chaminade softball team becomes surprise team in Mission League
plaintext:
Perhaps Chaminade’s softball team should no longer be considered a surprise for its 7-0 start overall and 1-0 mark in the Mission League.

Its 7-6 win over defending Mission League champion Sherman Oaks Notre Dame on Thursday featured a five-run seventh inning that should get everyone’s attention. Notre Dame went 8-0 in league play last season. Casey Glantz hit a two-run go-ahead home run.

Chaminade has featured sophomore pitcher Finley Suppan, the daughter of former major leaguer Jeff Supan. She’s 6-0 with a 1.15 ERA. She had a strikeout to end the game. The Eagles are flourishing under coach Cory Skinner.

Garden Grove Pacifica 4, Anaheim Canyon 1: Shay Kletke hit a home run and also pitched four innings.

Thousand Oaks 9, Granada Hills 4: Zoe Justman had three hits for Granada Hills.

Los Alamitos 6, Huntington Beach 5: A home run by Erin Denny on a 3-and-2 count in the top of the ninth inning broke a 5-5 tie and lifted the Griffins to victory.

Baseball

Sun Valley Poly 11, Sylmar 9: The Parrots improved to 3-0 in the Valley Mission League with a huge win over preseason favorite Sylmar.

Etiwanda 2, Foothill 0: Four Etiwanda pitchers combined on a three-hit shutout. Foothill had recorded shutout wins in four of its last five games. James Milam had two doubles.

La Mirada 7, Aliso Niguel 1: Blake Barberena had two hits and two RBIs.

Santa Monica 8, Calabasas 4: Jaxson Ehlers went four for four to lead Santa Monica.

context: James Milam HTD .

title: Tim Kelly: West Coast Eagles recall veteran midfielder for clash with North Melbourne Kangaroos
plaintext:
West Coast have recalled veteran midfielder Tim Kelly for their winnable clash against North Melbourne at Optus Stadium on Sunday.

Kelly was the shock omission from the Eagles’ season-opening loss to Gold Coast, travelling as an emergency.

The West Australian revealed the 31-year-old has earned a recall to take on the Kangaroos as part of a team that will also welcome back co-captain Liam Baker and key defender Harry Edwards.

West Coast have already dropped premiership defender Tom Cole after he struggled down back against the Suns, while draftee Josh Lindsay and defender Ryan Maric have also come out of the side.

Coach Andrew McQualter hinted early on Friday that more experience would be brought into the side to support their new stars.

“That’s exactly what we’re talking about internally. We’ve got a young squad, which everybody’s aware of, but we don’t want to throw them out to the wolves. So we’ve got to support them with the right them with the right maturity around them. And it’s going to be a balance every week. So that’s it, an ongoing discussion internally for us,” he said.

“We are aiming to pick the best team available every week to win, understanding that we can’t just pick a young team. So that’s the big thing.

“We learned that last year. When we were too young, we weren’t quite ready to compete. So there’s no specific plan around our young players. It’s just broadly the understanding that to play 23 games of AFL football for an 18-year-old is really tough.”
sentence: “That’s exactly what we’re talking about internally.
context: “ That ’s exactly what we ’re TAI .

title: Hawks star Nick Watson claims fan threw coke bottle at him at MCG
plaintext:
Hawks star Nick Watson claims a disgruntled Sydney Swans fan threw a projectile at him after Thursday’s clash at the MCG.

The Hawks soared over the Swans, winning 99-82 in front of a home crowd with Watson kicking the final goal.

Watson told Nova 100’s Jase & Lauren on Friday morning that he had engaged in a cheeky sledging match with a pack of Sydney supporters after the game when the bottle came flying out of the stands on to the field.

“They were throwing Coke Zero cans out there,” Watson said.

“I saw one of them go straight past me.

“It just landed right next to us, lucky it didn’t hit any players.

“I was giving (the Swans fans) heaps, but I am not sure a Coke Zero bottle to the face would have been nice.

Images released by the Herald Sun show the Coke bottle sailing over Watson’s head after the final siren.

Watson told Nova hosts Jase Hawkins and Lauren Phillips that he had no idea if any action had been taken against the man who threw the bottle.

“I don’t know what happened to the guy (who threw it),” he said.

The AFL has confirmed it is looking into the incident.
sentence: The Hawks soared over the Swans, winning 99-82 in front of a home crowd with Watson kicking the final goal.
context: The Hawks soared over the Swans , winning 99 - 82 in front of a home crowd with Watson kicking TFG .

title: Kings lose to Flyers in shootout, but move back into wild-card spot
plaintext:
Trevor Zegras and Matvei Michkov scored in a shootout to give the Philadelphia Flyers their fourth victory in five games, 4-3 over the Kings on Thursday night.

Noah Cates had a goal and an assist, Travis Konecny and Travis Sanheim also scored, and Samuel Ersson made 22 saves. The Flyers remained six points behind Boston and Detroit for the two Eastern Conference wild-card spots.

On Wednesday night against the Ducks, Cates scored in overtime in the Flyers’ 3-2 victory.

Adrian Kempe and Artemi Panarin failed on their shootout attempts for Los Angeles, though the Kings still moved into the second wild-card in the Western Conference.

Artemi Panarin had a goal and an assist for the Kings. Quinton Byfield and Anze Kopitar also scored, and Darcy Kuemper made 17 saves.

Panarin ensured the Kings picked up a point in the standings with a blistering wrist shot on a power play that tied it 3-3 with 9:32 remaining.

The Flyers were without forwards Sean Couturier (upper body), Luke Glendening (lower body) and Denver Barkey (upper body), leaving them to play with 11 forwards and seven defensemen.

Up next for the Kings: vs. Buffalo at Crypto.com Arena on Saturday.
sentence: Trevor Zegras and Matvei Michkov scored in a shootout to give the Philadelphia Flyers their fourth victory in five games, 4-3 over the Kings on Thursday night.
context: Trevor Zegras and Matvei Michkov scored IAS to give the Philadelphia Flyers their fourth victory in five games , 4 - 3 over the Kings on Thursday night .

title: Banksia Grove: Police investigating after woman found dead inside home
plaintext:
Forensic police officers remain at a northern suburbs home after a woman was found dead inside on Thursday morning.

WA Police are investigating if a 42-year-old woman was murdered after emergency services rushed to a home on Mayali Bend in Banksia Grove following reports of an “unresponsive” woman about 9am Thursday.

A man in his 40s is assisting police. No charges have been laid yet in relation to the woman’s death.

A forensics van and police car were parked in front of the house on Friday morning with the property still cordoned off by police tape.

“Rest in paradise to our neighbour. My heart goes out to you and your family in this shocking time,” one neighbour posted on social media overnight.

Another was also devastated by the woman’s death.

“She was always super nice, super friendly. Would always say hello, so yeah, it was just devastating, really,” neighbour Indi Hull said.

So it’s just really sad. I just feel for her and her family.”

It is understood the man currently assisting police and the woman were known to each other.

If you or someone you know is experiencing family violence, phone 1800 RESPECT (1800 737 732) or the Crisis Care Helpline on 1800 199 008.
sentence: “Rest in paradise to our neighbour.
context: “ Rest in paradise TON .

title: House That! Willetton in new price record as home - with a 1970s style exterior - smashes $3 million barrier
plaintext:
The property juggernaut does not get any wilder than the new price-record set in Willetton this month.

A family home with a 1970s exterior and modern extension has sold for $3.011 million, surpassing the previous $2.4m record and the suburb’s $1.35m median.

By way of comparison, a four-by-two family home a few streets from the river in Mosman Park sold last month for a cheaper price of only $2.9m.

Property sales agent Thomas Bale said the eye-watering sum in Willetton, 17km from the city, surprised many people, including other agents who had initially valued the home as being in the high $1 millions.

Bale had expected to sell it in the high $2 millions, based the home’s unique selling point as one of relatively few houses in the area with an extension built to a resort-style standard.

He said the quality of the home won over the buyers — even more so than the fact that it’s in the highly-coveted catchment areas for Rossmoyne High.

The front half of the home was built in the 1970s while the second half was added about a decade ago, creating a massive 329sqm of living space, with four bedrooms and two bathrooms.

Its renowned livability is due in part to a north-facing living room set alongside a lush pool and manicured gardens with an al fresco area with a pizza oven.

Inside, the breeze wafts through louvred windows, which enhances natural airflow and ventilation. The 850sqm block includes a two-car garage.
sentence: A family home with a 1970s exterior and modern extension has sold for $3.011 million, surpassing the previous $2.4m record and the suburb’s $1.35m median.
context: AFH with a 1970s exterior and modern extension has sold for $ 3.011 million , surpassing the previous $ 2.4 m record and the suburb ’s $ 1.35 m median .

title: RFDS Auxiliary raises funds to buy vital equipment for Kalgoorlie-Boulder base
plaintext:
The RFDS Eastern Goldfields Auxiliary has been working hard to raise funds to buy vital equipment for the Royal Flying Doctor Service’s Kalgoorlie base.

President Gemma Abbott said the auxiliary was “thrilled” to be able to buy a Credo Cube temperature-controlled medical transport bag.

Ms Abbot said the Credo Cube allowed vaccinations to be kept at the required temperature for travel on primary health clinic runs to stations and communities across the Goldfields.

“We also purchased two extra sets of Bose Aviation headsets to be used on the aircraft,” she said.

She said the total cost of these purchases was $6410.

“We could not have made these purchases without the generosity of the Goldfields community who donate to us though tins at spring festival and at businesses throughout Goldfields towns, raffles, and events we run such as the quiz night,” she said.

“Our next event will be Friday, May 8 to celebrate Royal Flying Doctor Day.

“We will be holding a raffle outside Coles Kalgoorlie.”
sentence: “Our next event will be Friday, May 8 to celebrate Royal Flying Doctor Day.
context: “ ONE will be Friday , May 8 to celebrate Royal Flying Doctor Day .

title: MLG Oz wins two-year contract extension at Northern Star’s Jundee gold mine worth $50 million
plaintext:
Kalgoorlie-Boulder mining services firm MLG Oz has won a second contract extension in as many days, this time with Northern Star Resources.

MLG told the Australian Securities Exchange on Thursday the two-year agreement for the Jundee gold mine in the northern Goldfields would earn the company about $50 million in revenue.

Under the agreement, MLG will provide a range of integrated site services.

These include surface haulage of ore and waste material, ROM material handling to ensure a continuous supply of ore to the processing plant, and stockpiling and re-handling pad management.

MLG will also undertake waste dump management, construction and maintenance of landfill and bioremediation cells, maintenance of surface haul roads including watering and dust suppression, and the ongoing maintenance of road signs and edge markers across operational routes.

The Jundee contract follows MLG receiving a $75m year-long extension of its work with Westgold Resources at that company’s hubs at Cue, Meekatharra, and Fortnum.

MLG acting chief executive Mark Hatfield said the company was pleased to continue supporting Northern Star at the Jundee operation.

“The continuation of these services reflects the strong operational performance of our team on site and demonstrates the value of MLG’s integrated service offering in supporting our clients’ production activities,” he said.
sentence: MLG told the Australian Securities Exchange on Thursday the two-year agreement for the Jundee gold mine in the northern Goldfields would earn the company about $50 million in revenue.
context: MLG told the Australian Securities Exchange on Thursday the two - year agreement for the Jundee gold mine in TNG would earn the company about $ 50 million in revenue .

title: Florida hospital sues to evict patient who refuses to leave for months
plaintext:
A Florida hospital is suing a former patient who it says has refused to leave for five months after being medically cleared and formally discharged.

Tallahassee Memorial HealthCare (TMH) filed the complaint earlier this month, saying the woman was formally discharged on Oct. 6, 2025, but "continues to occupy" an inpatient room.

"TMH staff made repeated efforts to assist the defendant in safely completing discharge," the complaint obtained by the Tallahassee Democrat says. "TMH offered assistance, including coordination with family members and offering non-emergency medical transportation to obtain necessary identification."

Hospital officials say the prolonged stay is straining limited resources and preventing other patients from receiving care.

"TMH has limited inpatient beds," the complaint states. "The defendant’s continued occupancy prevents use of the bed for patients needing acute care."

Despite its efforts, the woman remained in the hospital as of March, according to the filing.

TMH is now seeking a court order requiring her to leave and authorizing the Leon County Sheriff’s Office to assist in removing her if necessary.

A hearing is scheduled for March 30.

It is unclear how often hospitals resort to legal action to remove patients who refuse to leave after discharge.

TMH told Fox News Digital in an email requesting comment on the matter that "TMH is not able to discuss active legal matters, including background details."
sentence: "The defendant’s continued occupancy prevents use of the bed for patients needing acute care.
context: " The defendant ’s continued occupancy prevents use of the bed for patients NAC .

title: Iran calls for regional coordination in calls with Turkey, Egypt, Pakistan
plaintext:
Iran's foreign minister called for vigilance and regional coordination in separate calls with counterparts in Turkey, Egypt and Pakistan as the military warned of a tougher response to any further attacks on its energy infrastructure, state media reported on Thursday.

After its energy facilities in South Pars gas field and Asaluyeh were targeted on Wednesday, Iran retaliated against what it said were U.S.-linked energy sites in Gulf countries, including Ras Laffan Industrial City in Qatar, the world’s largest LNG complex.

"In the calls to foreign counterparts, Araqchi assessed the U.S. and Zionist regime's (Israel) attack on Iranian infrastructure as an act aimed at escalating tensions and destabilising the region, and urged for vigilance and coordination among regional countries in response to these threats," state media reported on Thursday.

A spokesman for the unified command of Iran's armed forces said recent strikes on the country's energy infrastructure led to "a new stage in the war" in which Iran targeted energy facilities linked to the U.S. and American investors in the region.

"If strikes (on Iran's energy infrastructure) happen again, further attacks on your energy infrastructure and that of your allies will not stop until it is completely destroyed, and our response will be much more severe," Ebrahim Zolfaqari said according to state media.

(Reporting by Dubai Newsroom; Editing by Alison Williams, Alexandra Hudson)
sentence: "In the calls to foreign counterparts, Araqchi assessed the U.S. and Zionist regime's (Israel) attack on Iranian infrastructure as an act aimed at escalating tensions and destabilising the region, and urged for vigilance and coordination among regional countries in response to these threats," state media reported on Thursday.
context: " In the calls to foreign counterparts , Araqchi assessed the U.S. and Zionist regime 's ( Israel ) attack on Iranian infrastructure as an act aimed at escalating tensions and DTR , and urged for vigilance and coordination among regional countries in response to these threats , " state media reported on Thursday .

title: Spacey settles UK lawsuits over alleged sex assault
plaintext:
Oscar-winning US actor Kevin Spacey has reached a confidential settlement with three British men who had filed civil lawsuits at London's High Court accusing him of sexual assault between 2000 and 2013, according to a court order made public this week.

Two of the claimants were complainants who gave evidence during Spacey's 2023 criminal trial in London, where the now 66-year-old actor was acquitted of all charges.

Spacey, one of Hollywood's biggest stars before he was first accused of sexual assault in 2017, has consistently denied accusations of sexual misconduct and said the incidents alleged in the lawsuits did not happen or were consensual.

A court order dated March 13 said the lawsuits have been stayed following the settlement, meaning a trial that had been scheduled for October will not take place.

Spacey's lawyers did not immediately respond to a request for comment. A spokesperson for the claimants' lawyers declined to comment.

Spacey, who won Oscars for American Beauty and The Usual Suspects, was dropped from the TV drama House of Cards and removed from the movie All the Money in the World after the first allegations of sexual assault emerged.

A civil lawsuit against Spacey in the United States was dismissed by a jury in 2022, and he stood trial in London the following year, charged with sexually assaulting four men in Britain. He was acquitted of all nine charges.

1800 RESPECT (1800 737 732)

National Sexual Abuse and Redress Support Service 1800 211 028

title: US detects drones over base where Rubio, Hegseth live
plaintext:
US officials detected unidentified drones above an army base in Washington where secretary of state Marco Rubio and Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth live, the Washington Post reported on Wednesday, citing three people briefed on the situation.

The officials have not determined where the drones came from, the report said, citing two of the people.

The drones over Fort McNair prompted officials to consider relocating Rubio and Hegseth, the report said.

However, the secretaries have not moved, the report added, citing a senior administration official.

The newspaper said the US military was monitoring potential threats more closely because of the heightened alert level over the US and Israeli war against Iran.

Reuters could not independently verify the report immediately.

The Pentagon and the US state department did not respond to requests for comment.

Chief Pentagon spokesperson Sean Parnell declined to discuss the drones with the Washington Post.

“The department cannot comment on the secretary’s (Hegseth’s) movements for security reasons, and reporting on such movements is grossly irresponsible,” he told the Post.
sentence: The drones over Fort McNair prompted officials to consider relocating Rubio and Hegseth, the report said.
context: The drones over Fort McNair prompted officials to consider relocating RAH , the report said .

title: Teacher to remain in jail after allegedly raping learner at Limpopo school
plaintext:
The teacher appeared before the Mahwelereng Magistrate's Court.

A 56-year-old male teacher has been remanded in custody following his court appearance in connection with the alleged rape of an 18-year-old learner.

The teacher appeared before the Mahwelereng Magistrate’s Court on Wednesday, where the case was postponed to Wednesday, 25 March, for further investigation.

Arrest

Police spokesperson Colonel Malesela Ledwaba said the suspect was arrested following an incident that allegedly occurred at a secondary school in the Mahwelereng policing area.

“It is alleged that the learner was previously confronted by an educator after being suspected of using a cellphone during an examination. The cellphone was confiscated, and she was later instructed to return with money in order to retrieve it.

“Upon her return, the educator allegedly instructed the learner to follow him to a restroom in the premises where the incident reportedly occurred,” Ledwaba said.

Ledwaba added that the Provincial Commissioner of Police in Limpopo, Lieutenant General Thembi Hadebe, reiterated the police’s commitment to ensuring that perpetrators of gender-based violence are brought to justice.
sentence: “Upon her return, the educator allegedly instructed the learner to follow him to a restroom in the premises where the incident reportedly occurred,” Ledwaba said.
context: “ UHR , the educator allegedly instructed the learner to follow him to a restroom in the premises where the incident reportedly occurred , ” Ledwaba said .

title: Person allegedly stabbed with ‘cutting instrument’ outside Albany Justice Complex, bloodied area cordoned off
plaintext:
A person has allegedly been stabbed outside the Albany Justice Complex, with police cordoning off an area where a large blood spatter can be seen on the pavement.

The alleged attack occurred just after 2pm on Thursday outside the courthouse on Stirling Terrace.

Albany-officer-in-charge Sen. Sgt Carlos Correia said police believe the two people are known to each other.

“There was a bit of commotion between two people, which are believed to be known to each other, when one allegedly produced a cutting instrument.” he said.

“Police are conducting further enquiries as to the circumstances of the incident.”

He said both people had left the scene and officers were working to confirm their identities and relationship to each other.

Sen Sgt Correia said the person’s injuries were not believed to be life-threatening.

Officers cordoned off the bloodied area of the ramp walkway with police tape and could be seen talking to people and courthouse orderlies who may have witnessed the incident.

Forensics officers arrived at about 3pm to examine and photograph the scene.
sentence: A person has allegedly been stabbed outside the Albany Justice Complex, with police cordoning off an area where a large blood spatter can be seen on the pavement.
context: A person has allegedly been stabbed outside the Albany Justice Complex , with police cordoning off an area where a large blood spatter can be seen OTP .

title: Former counterterrorism chief Joe Kent under FBI investigation for alleged classified leaks
plaintext:
The FBI is investigating former National Counterterrorism Center Director Joe Kent over allegations that he leaked classified information, according to three sources familiar with the matter.

The probe predates Kent’s departure from government, sources told Fox News Digital.

The FBI declined Fox News' request for comment.

Fox News Digital has reached out to Kent by phone and email for comment.

Kent resigned Tuesday from his role as the nation’s top counterterrorism official, citing opposition to the U.S. war against Iran.

In a public statement, Kent said he "cannot in good conscience support the ongoing war," arguing that Iran posed no imminent threat and that the conflict marked a break from the administration’s prior approach to avoiding prolonged wars in the Middle East.

A senior administration official previously told Fox News Digital that Kent was a "known leaker" who had been cut out of intelligence briefings months before his resignation.

It is unclear whether the FBI investigation is related to those concerns or to a separate matter.

Kent, a former Army Green Beret and CIA paramilitary officer, was appointed to lead the National Counterterrorism Center in early 2025. He is a Gold Star spouse.
sentence: Kent, a former Army Green Beret and CIA paramilitary officer, was appointed to lead the National Counterterrorism Center in early 2025.
context: Kent , a former AGB and CIA paramilitary officer , was appointed to lead the National Counterterrorism Center in early 2025 .

title: Jetstar announces 60k cheap fares to Aussie beaches in snap ‘Life’s a Beach Sale’
plaintext:
A major Aussie airline has released tens of thousands of discounted airfares to and from idyllic coastal destinations across the country.

Jetstar’s “Life’s a Beach Sale” launched on Thursday, promising to encourage “holiday-makers to swap the everyday for sand, sea and sunshine” by introducing 60,000 discounted seats across domestic and international routes.

“The Life’s a Beach Sale features low fares across some of Australia’s most popular beach destinations, including the Sunshine Coast, Gold Coast, Cairns, Whitsundays Coast, and Hervy Bay,” a Jetstar spokesman said.

Some example flights include Adelaide to the Sunshine Coast from $49, Cairns to the Gold Coast from $49 and Sydney to Hervey Bay from $49.

Melbourne residents keen for a trip to the Whitsundays can take a scenic trip for $49 and even those hoping to do a little bit of travel through the Pacific have been given a leg-up with $189 tickets from Sydney to Vanuatu.

Jetstar executive customer and digital manager Prue McKenzie said the sale was “the perfect excuse” to plan a holiday.

“Nothing beats a beach holiday – and with fares starting from $49, this sale is the perfect excuse to plan your next escape and take off to some much-loved coastal destinations,” Ms McKenzie said.

“Jetstar is committed to always offering low fares so more people can travel more often, and sales like this help make those spontaneous beach holidays a reality.”

More to come
sentence: “The Life’s a Beach Sale features low fares across some of Australia’s most popular beach destinations, including the Sunshine Coast, Gold Coast, Cairns, Whitsundays Coast, and Hervy Bay,” a Jetstar spokesman said.
context: “ The Life ’s a Beach Sale features low fares across some of Australia ’s most popular beach destinations , including the Sunshine Coast , Gold Coast , Cairns , Whitsundays Coast , and Hervy Bay , ” AJS said .

title: Argentina to lead South America bid for Rugby World Cup
plaintext:
Argentina will bid to stage the 2035 World Cup as part of a wider South American tournament that will also include Brazil, Chile and Uruguay.

World Rugby chief executive Alan Gilpin is visiting the home of the Pumas to assess their feasibility as hosts for the event that will take place after Australia host the 2027 tournament and USA the 2031 RWC.

"The 2035 bid represents a federal objective and a legacy project that transcends our borders," said Gabriel Travaglini, president of Argentina's rugby union.

"Welcoming Alan Gilpin to advance this technical analysis is a fundamental step in demonstrating that the region is ready.

"We want a World Cup that reflects the passion and development that rugby has achieved in every corner of our territory."

Formal applications for hosting rights will be submitted this autumn with a preferred host identified in May 2027 before World Rugby's council gives final approval the following November.

Argentina would be a popular destination for the 13th edition of the World Cup and worthy hosts given they have reached the semi-finals three times. They are currently positioned fifth in the global rankings, one place above England.

Japan, Spain, Italy and a combined Middle East bid are shaping up to be their rivals to put on the tournament.
sentence: "We want a World Cup that reflects the passion and development that rugby has achieved in every corner of our territory.
context: " We want AWC that reflects the passion and development that rugby has achieved in every corner of our territory .

title: KwaMashu school teachers robbed, forced to transfer cash
plaintext:
Armed suspects who stormed Zamokuhle Primary School in KwaMashu, north of Durban, on Friday robbed teachers of their belongings and forced them to transfer money from their bank accounts.

KwaZulu-Natal education MEC Sipho Hlomuka condemned the incident.

“This is a barbaric and cowardly act that targets those who have dedicated their lives to shaping the future of our country. Our teachers must be protected at all times and should never have to work under the threat of violence,” said Hlomuka.

He said the incident left teachers and pupils deeply traumatised.

Hlomuka said the department was working with police, who opened a case of armed robbery after the attack.

Psychosocial support was provided to affected teachers and pupils, and safety and security measures are being urgently strengthened at the school and surrounding institutions.

“This is a direct assault on the future of our country and our province. We cannot allow criminals to turn our schools into crime scenes. Schools must remain safe havens for teaching and learning. We call on communities to work with law enforcement and the department to protect our schools,” Hlomuka said.

He said the department is “deeply concerned about increasing incidents of violence in and around schools” and reiterated the call for the public to help protect schools, teachers and pupils.
sentence: KwaZulu-Natal education MEC Sipho Hlomuka condemned the incident.
context: KwaZulu - Natal education MEC Sipho Hlomuka CTI .

title: Child murdered after woman ‘took him for prayers at riverbank’ in Mpumalanga
plaintext:
The police in Embalenhle, Mpumalanga, have arrested a 34-year-old woman in connection with the murder of a 10-year-old boy.

Col Mavela Masondo said the woman had taken the child on Monday “under the pretext of going to pray in an open field.

“She later returned and reported the child missing. A search operation was immediately conducted by the police, community members and a private security company, which led to the discovery of the child’s body a few metres from the riverbank in Azania, Embalenhle.

“Crime scene and forensic experts from SAPS attended the scene and processed it accordingly.”

Masondo said the woman is expected to appear before the Evander magistrate’s court on Wednesday, facing a charge of murder.

“The South African Police Service has strongly condemned this senseless act of violence against a child and continues to prioritise crimes against women and children in line with the National Strategic Plan on Gender-Based Violence and Femicide.

“Community members are urged to remain calm and allow the law to take its course.”
sentence: “She later returned and reported the child missing.
context: “ She later returned and reported TCM .

title: Pick of the Roses: fourth Aussie joins Scottish outfit
plaintext:
Newcastle Jets attacker Lachlan Rose will join Scotland's Dundee United on a two-year deal at the end of the A-League Men's season.

The in-form 26-year-old has signed a pre-contract agreement with The Terrors, with a club-held option of a further 12 months beyond 2028.

Rose is equal-third in the golden boot race with eight goals from 21 games, behind Auckland duo Sam Cosgrove (10) and future teammate Jesse Randall (nine), the New Zealander also signing a pre-contract deal at Tannadice in January.

Rose will link with six-cap Socceroos defender Ryan Strain, former Western Sydney Wanderers striker Zac Sapsford and England-born goalkeeper Ashley Maynard-Brewer as the fourth Australian contracted to United.

Rose is enjoying his most productive seasons at the Jets after signing from Macarthur FC in 2024, matching the goal tally he set last season with five rounds remaining before the ALM finals.

"While we are disappointed to lose Lachie, we are delighted for him that he has earned this opportunity to test himself in Europe," Newcastle's football manager Nigel Boogard said.

"Opportunities like this are what we want for our players and Lachie has put himself in this position through his work and commitment."

Rose has scored 16 goals in 39 games for Newcastle, currently perched one point above Auckland FC, and become something of a cult hero among Novocastrians by helping place the Jets on the cusp of a maiden premiership, having finished runners-up in 2008 and 2018.
sentence: "Opportunities like this are what we want for our players and Lachie has put himself in this position through his work and commitment.
context: " OLT are what we want for our players and Lachie has put himself in this position through his work and commitment .

title: Use of ankle bracelets to track immigration detainees struck down by High Court
plaintext:
The High Court has ruled against the use of ankle monitoring devices and curfews to track immigration detainees.

Under new regulations imposed by the Federal Government in the wake of the NZYQ case, former detainees were required to comply with the strict restrictions.

A Papua New Guinea citizen successfully challenged the legality of the new measures in the High Court, resulting in them being struck down in Canberra on Wednesday

The ruling — which found they weren’t compatible with the constitution — came after the Federal Government’s initial regime for monitoring was also thrown out as unconstitutional in 2024.

Labor had rushed through emergency laws to impose the condition after the High Court’s bombshell NZYQ ruling in late 2023.

At the time had allowed more than 80 ex-detainees to be released and sparked community concern about the cohort of mainly convicted criminals.

Hundreds of detainees have been released since, dozens of which have further breached visa conditions.

More to come.
sentence: The High Court has ruled against the use of ankle monitoring devices and curfews to track immigration detainees.
context: The High Court has ruled against the use of ankle monitoring devices and curfews to TID .

title: Iran Guards confirm death of Basij paramilitary chief in US-Israeli strike
plaintext:
Iran's Revolutionary Guards on Tuesday confirmed the death of the commander of the affiliated Basij paramilitary force in an U.S.-Israeli strike.

"Commander Gholamreza Soleimani, head of the Basij Organization, has been martyred," the Guards said on their Sepah News website, after Israel said it had killed him in an airstrike.
sentence: "Commander Gholamreza Soleimani, head of the Basij Organization, has been martyred," the Guards said on their Sepah News website, after Israel said it had killed him in an airstrike.
context: " Commander Gholamreza Soleimani , head of TBO , has been martyred , " the Guards said on their Sepah News website , after Israel said it had killed him in an airstrike .

title: Kyle Sandilands sacked by ARN, says it is ‘over to his lawyers’
plaintext:
Kyle Sandilands has been sacked by ARN Media in the fallout of his on-air clash with co-host Jackie O, with the radio giant saying he does not accept the outcome and it is now “over to my lawyers”.

Sandilands confirmed his lucrative contract had been terminated in a statement on Wednesday morning.

“My lawyers told them last week this would be invalid. And guess what? It is,” he said.

“Let me tell you what actually happened here. Jackie and I had a blue on air. That’s it. The kind of thing we’ve done a hundred times in 25 years.”

In late February 2026, Mr Sandilands was suspended from KIIS FM following an on-air argument with Ms Henderson which resulted in her leaving the show mid-recording and subsequently failing to appear on-air for more than a week.

Mr Sandilands was given two weeks by KIIS parent company Australian Radio Network (ARN) to “remedy” the feud, under threat of losing his job.

On March 6, Ms Henderson released a statement clarifying she had not actually quit.

More to come
sentence: “Let me tell you what actually happened here.
context: “ Let me tell you what AHH .

title: Vision of three men and a car near alleged site of Chris Baghsarian’s murder
plaintext:
Vision has emerged of three men lurking in the area where Chris Baghsarian is believed to have been taken before his alleged murder in February.

The 85-year-old was allegedly kidnapped on February 13 in what NSW Police believe was a case of mistaken identity before his remains were eventually located near a golf club in Pitt Town in Sydney’s northwest on February 24.

Police believe Mr Baghsarian was held at a “stronghold” in Dural, about 25km southeast of Pitt Town, for an unknown period of time before his remains were eventually found.

Two men, Gerard Anthony Andrews, 29, and Daniel Stevens, 24, have been charged with Mr Baghsarian’s kidnapping and murder.

Police have released CCTV footage to the public in an effort to identify a group of men who were spotted in the area where the remains of kidnapping victim Chris Baghsarian were found.

A police spokesman said there were “an additional three men who may be able to assist with inquiries”.

“The men are captured on CCTV walking towards the kidnappers’ stronghold on Wildthorn Ave in Dural on the night of the kidnapping. The next day, a blue Hyundai sedan is seen arriving and then leaving the same location just after 12pm,” the spokesman said.

“Of note, the blue Hyundai is missing a hubcap on its left side. Anyone with information about the identity of the men captured in the CCTV or the driver of the blue car is urged to contact Crime Stoppers.”

Mr Andrews and Mr Stevens remain before the courts.

More to come
sentence: A police spokesman said there were “an additional three men who may be able to assist with inquiries”.
context: A police spokesman said there were “ an additional three men who may be able to AWI ” .

title: Robinson, Badela reunite with Mokwena in Libya
plaintext:
Having been cleared to leave Algeria after he was detained on Sunday, former Mamelodi Sundowns, Wydad Casablanca and Orlando Pirates coach Rulani Mokwena has arrived in Libya to start his new life with Al Ittihad Tripoli.

Sowetan reported Mokwena was on Monday given a suspended sentence for violating Algeria’s currency regulations. The 39-year-old will be joined by Sinethemba Badela and Wendell Robinson, who will be part of his technical team again.

Robinson worked with Mokwena at MC Alger this season before Mokwena announced his surprise departure at the weekend, while he also worked with Badela at Wydad.

Mokwena has been given freedom to bring his trusted backroom staff. He is also set to link up with former player Thembinkosi Lorch, who he worked with at Pirates, Sundowns and Wydad.

The department of international relations, meanwhile, confirmed the talented coach was freed after detention over possession of foreign currency.

“The SA Embassy in Algeria has confirmed that Mokwena has been cleared to leave Algeria. He is at a hotel. Our team will be with him tomorrow to ensure that his travel back home is facilitated without hiccups,” said Clayson Monyela, SA’s head of public diplomacy, in an X which he later updated: “Coach Mokwena is now safely in Libya. He decided to proceed to Libya where he’s pursuing new opportunities.”
sentence: Having been cleared to leave Algeria after he was detained on Sunday, former Mamelodi Sundowns, Wydad Casablanca and Orlando Pirates coach Rulani Mokwena has arrived in Libya to start his new life with Al Ittihad Tripoli.
context: Having been cleared to leave Algeria after he was detained on Sunday , former Mamelodi Sundowns , Wydad Casablanca and Orlando Pirates coach Rulani Mokwena has arrived in Libya to start HNL with Al Ittihad Tripoli .

title: Mahindra Pik Up the surprise of February’s best-selling bakkies
plaintext:
Pik Up recorded one of its best sales months in recent memory.

In a month that saw it sold 10 000 units of the XUV 3X0, Mahindra also emerged as the biggest surprise of February’s best-selling bakkies.

Record

Combined with the 592 XUV 3X0’s sold, the locally built Pik Up amassed 1 003 unit sales, one of its highest in recent memory.

A result that placed its 13th overall on the list of overall vehicle sales for the month, the Pik Up placed fourth behind the Isuzu D-Max.

With the Toyota Hilux finishing first and the Ford Ranger second, locally produced bakkies also occupied four places within the top five.

A distance behind the Pik Up, the GWM P-Series, consisting of the P300 and P500, placed fifth ahead of the Toyota Land Cruiser 79 and Volkswagen Amarok.

The Nissan Navara ended February in eighth, followed by two further Chinese entrants in the GWM Steed and JAC T-Series.

February’s best-selling bakkies:

The complete list of February’s best-selling bakkies are as follows:

Still on the up

In total, new vehicle sales jumped 11.4% in February from the same time 12 months ago to 53 455.

Of these, light commercial vehicle sales contributed 13 218 units, an uptake of 11.9% from last year’s 11 816.
sentence: With the Toyota Hilux finishing first and the Ford Ranger second, locally produced bakkies also occupied four places within the top five.
context: With the Toyota Hilux finishing first and TFR second , locally produced bakkies also occupied four places within the top five .

title: Significant Israeli ground offensive in Lebanon must be averted: Western leaders statement
plaintext:
The leaders of five Western countries said in a joint statement Monday that a large-scale Israeli ground operation in Lebanon "must be averted."

"A significant Israeli ground offensive would have devastating humanitarian consequences and could lead to a protracted conflict," said the joint statement from the leaders of Canada, France, Germany, Italy and the United Kingdom.
